title: Hybridization of Ferromagnetic and Cyclotron Resonances in a Two-Dimensional Electron System on a Ferromagnetic Film
authors: A. A. Zabolotnykh, I. V. Zagorodnev, A. A. Matveev, D. A. Rodionov, O. Yu. Arkhipova, D. V. Kalyabin, A. R. Safin, S. A. Nikitov
abstract: The microwave response of a two-dimensional (2D) electron system located on a dielectric ferromagnetic film, which in turn lies on a conducting metal (gate), has been theoretically studied. The entire system has been placed in the perpendicular static magnetic field. It has been found that the ferromagnetic resonance of the film and the cyclotron resonance of the electrons of the 2D system interact in the magnetic field, leading to their repulsion ('anticrossing'). It has been revealed that the anticrossing region is characterized not only by the modification of resonance frequencies compared to the cyclotron resonance in the 2D system without the ferromagnetic substrate and the ferromagnetic resonance in the film without the 2D system, but also by a strong change in the resonance linewidths.
summary: This paper theoretically explores how the magnetic properties of a ferromagnetic film can be coupled to the electronic properties of a 2D electron system. It demonstrates that this coupling leads to the splitting of resonance frequencies and the creation of Fano-type interference. This work is significant for designing tunable hybrid magneto-electronic devices.
main_problem: The study investigates the hybridization and interaction between ferromagnetic resonance (FMR) and cyclotron resonance (CR) in a hybrid heterostructure.
main_result: The researchers found that the FMR and CR undergo anticrossing (frequency repulsion) and identified the emergence of Fano-type antiresonance, accompanied by significant changes in resonance linewidths.
method: The authors use a theoretical approach based on electromagnetic boundary conditions at the interfaces and the Drude model for electronic conductivity to derive the reflection and absorption coefficients.
model_or_system: A 2D electron system (2DES) is placed on a dielectric ferromagnetic film (Bi:TmIG) which sits atop a conducting metal gate, all subjected to a perpendicular static magnetic field.
key_observables: Amplitude reflection coefficient (r), energy absorption coefficient (A), resonance frequencies, and resonance linewidths.
important_parameters: Magnetic field (H), electron density (n), film thickness (d), saturation magnetization (Ms), gyromagnetic ratio (gamma), and Gilbert damping (alpha).
main_assumptions: The system is treated in the Fabry-Perot limit (low frequency relative to cavity frequency), and the ferromagnetic film is assumed to have frequency-independent permittivity and perpendicular magnetic anisotropy.
figures_summary: Figure 1 provides a schematic of the heterostructure; Figure 2 shows the frequency dependence of the hybridized resonance branches and their splitting versus the applied magnetic field.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the physical system, defines the electromagnetic and electronic models, derives the analytical expressions for resonance interaction, and concludes with a discussion of the anticrossing and antiresonance regimes.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

The damping coeﬀicient of the film is α = 10−2, the electron relaxation time in the 2D system is τ = 200 ps, and the remaining parameters are the same as in the caption to Fig. 2.

Spintronics.note: The paper directly studies the interface of magnetism and electronics through the coupling of magnons (FMR) and 2D electron dynamics (CR) in a heterostructure, specifically demonstrating Fano-type interference and mode hybridization.
